initial_program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine which nonzero cells belong to the main component.
% (The component that contains the cell with the smallest row and column
%  will be the one that defines the output; other small components are
%  ignored automatically by the borderselection logic below.)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
nonZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of the main component (the largest component).
minRow(N) :- N = #min{R : nonZero(R,_) }.
maxRow(N) :- N = #max{R : nonZero(R,_) }.
minCol(M) :- M = #min{C : nonZero(_,C)}.
maxCol(M) :- M = #max{C : nonZero(_,C)}.

% For each cell compute its layer  the distance to the nearest border.
% The layer is the smallest of the four distances to the rectangle edges.
layer(R,C,L) :- nonZero(R,C,_), 
    L = #min{L : (L = R - minRow) | (L = maxRow - R) | (L = C - minCol) | (L = maxCol - C)}.

% Output cells that lie on the border of their layer.
% These are exactly the cells that appear in the required output picture.
output(R_out,C_out,Color) :-
    nonZero(R,C,Color), layer(R,C,L),
    (R - minRow = L) # (maxRow - R = L) # (C - minCol = L) # (maxCol - C = L),
    R_out = R - minRow,
    C_out = C - minCol.

% Exactly one output colour for every output cell.
output_cell(R_out,C_out) :- input(R_out,C_out,_).
1 { output(R_out,C_out,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_out,C_out).

% Zero cells must stay zero.
zero(R_out,C_out) :- input(R_out,C_out,0).
output(R_out,C_out,0) :- zero(R_out,C_out).

#show output/3.

% Determine which nonzero cells belong to the main component.

nonZero(R,C) :- input(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% Bounding rectangle of the main component (the largest component).

maxRow(N) :- N = #max{R : nonZero(R,_) }.
minCol(M) :- M = #min{C : nonZero(_,C)}.
maxCol(M) :- M = #max{C : nonZero(_,C)}.

% For each cell compute its layer  the distance to the nearest border.
% The layer is the smallest of the four distances to the rectangle edges.
layer(R,C,L) :- nonZero(R,C,_), 
    L = #min{L : (L = R - minRow) | (L = maxRow - R) | (L = C - minCol) | (L = maxCol - C)}.

% Output cells that lie on the border of their layer.
% These are exactly the cells that appear in the required output picture.

    nonZero(R,C,Color), layer(R,C,L),
    (R - minRow = L) # (maxRow - R = L) # (C - minCol = L) # (maxCol - C = L),

    C_out = C - minCol.

% Exactly one output colour for every output cell.

1 { output(R_out,C_out,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R_out,C_out).

% Zero cells must stay zero.
zero(R_out,C_out) :- input(R_out,C_out,0).
